title: EDHOC: CBOR encoding errors silently ignored in build_sig_structure
description:
SECURITY/CORRECTNESS: build_sig_structure() ignores all CBOR encoding return values. If encoding fails (e.g., buffer too small), the function continues with corrupted data and cannot report failure.

Location: lichen/subsys/lichen/edhoc/edhoc.c:222-252

Issues:
- Line 231: zcbor_bstr_encode_ptr(zse_ext, th, 32) - no error check
- Line 232: zcbor_bstr_encode_ptr(zse_ext, cred, cred_len) - no error check
- Line 238: zcbor_bstr_encode_ptr(zse_bp, id_cred, id_cred_len) - no error check
- Line 243: zcbor_list_start_encode - no error check
- Lines 244-248: multiple zcbor calls without checks

Impact: Signature verification may operate on malformed data, potentially causing:
1. Signature verification to fail unexpectedly
2. Security bypass if malformed structures happen to verify

Fix: Check all zcbor return values, return error if any encoding fails.

File: lichen/subsys/lichen/edhoc/edhoc.c:222-252
close_reason: Fixed: Added CBOR encoding error checks in build_sig_structure(). All zcbor calls now check return values and propagate -EINVAL on failure. Updated all 4 callers to check return value and goto err_wipe on failure. Added SECURITY comment explaining the vulnerability. EC2 build verification not possible - SSH key missing.
